title: Confirm a student’s educational status for NIS
description: Used to keep paying a Survivors’ or Death Benefit to a student aged 16 to 25.
summary: If a child or young adult is receiving a Survivors’ Benefit or a Death Benefit and they are still in full-time education between the ages of 16 and 25, the benefit can continue. The National Insurance Office needs proof of their student status to keep paying.
The Educational Status Form is that proof. It is completed and stamped by the school, college or university, then sent to the National Insurance Office.
You will normally need a new form every academic year. The Office will pause payments if a form has not come in.
If the student takes a break from study, finishes their course, or starts working full-time, the benefit ends. Tell the National Insurance Office promptly.

items: the student’s name, NIS number and date of birth
the name and address of the school, college or university
the course the student is on and when it ends

items.name: A signed and stamped letter from the school
items.note: Confirming the student is enrolled in full-time education.

items.name: National ID for the parent or guardian

how_long: Confirmation is recorded within 2 weeks. You should send a new form for each new academic year.

options.heading: In person or by post
content: Take or send the completed Educational Status Form to the National Insurance Office.

assumptions: ‘Aged 16 to 25’ is the typical range for student survivors’ benefit eligibility. The form does not state the age range.
‘Confirmation is recorded within 2 weeks’ is an estimate.
‘New form for each new academic year’ is general practice, not explicit on the form.
